renderer.c(3) Library Functions Manual renderer.c(3)

NAME

renderer.c - render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct render_file_ctx_s

Functions


asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t **out)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, void *audio_media_source, void *audio_capture_rb)
asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, const char *ansi_frame, uint64_t captured_ns)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t *ctx)

Detailed Description

render_file_* — ties together font resolution, platform renderer, FFmpeg encoder

Definition in file renderer.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char * output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t ** out)

Definition at line 28 of file renderer.c.

29                                                               {
30   log_info("[RENDER_FILE_CREATE] CALLED with output_path=%s, cols=%d, rows=%d, fps=%d, theme=%d, out=%p",
31            output_path ? output_path : "(null)", cols, rows, fps, theme, (void *)out);
32   render_file_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*ctx), render_file_ctx_t *);
33 
34   // Resolve font spec → platform-appropriate file path, family name, or bundled data.
35   char font_spec[512] = {0};
36   bool font_is_path = false;
37   const uint8_t *font_data = NULL;
38   size_t font_data_size = 0;
39   const char *raw_font = GET_OPTION(render_font);
40 
41   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set, unless user explicitly overrides with --render-font
42   if ((!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
43     raw_font = "matrix";
44     log_debug("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Using matrix font (enabled by --matrix flag)");
45   }
46 
47   // BEFORE resolving fonts, check if matrix font and disable it for render-file (causes glyph height=0)
48   if (raw_font && strcmp(raw_font, "matrix") == 0) {
49     log_warn("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Matrix font not supported for render-file output, using system font instead");
50     raw_font = NULL;  // Force fallback to system font
51   }
52 
53   asciichat_error_t fe =
54       platform_font_resolve(raw_font, font_spec, sizeof(font_spec), &font_is_path, &font_data, &font_data_size);
55   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
56     log_warn("renderer: font resolution failed for '%s' — using system default", raw_font ? raw_font : "(explicit system)");
57   }
58 
59   log_debug("render_file_create: Font resolved: font_spec='%s', font_is_path=%d, font_data=%p (size=%zu)", font_spec,
60             font_is_path, (void *)font_data, font_data_size);
61 
62   // Use reasonable default font size for render-file quality
63   double font_size_pt = GET_OPTION(render_font_size);
64 
65   term_renderer_config_t tr_cfg = {
66       .cols = cols,
67       .rows = rows,
68       .font_size_pt = font_size_pt,
69       .theme = (term_renderer_theme_t)theme,
70       .font_is_path = font_is_path,
71       .font_data = font_data,
72       .font_data_size = font_data_size,
73   };
74   strncpy(tr_cfg.font_spec, font_spec, sizeof(tr_cfg.font_spec) - 1);
75 
76   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_create(&tr_cfg, &ctx->renderer);
77   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
78     log_error("render_file_create: term_renderer_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
79     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
80     return err;
81   }
82   int actual_width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
83   int actual_height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
84   log_info("render_file_create: GRID DIMENSIONS: %ux%u cells -> PIXEL DIMENSIONS: %dx%d px", cols, rows,
85            actual_width_px, actual_height_px);
86   log_debug("render_file_create: term_renderer created (%dx%d cells, %dx%d px)", cols, rows, actual_width_px,
87             actual_height_px);
88 
89   err = ffmpeg_encoder_create(output_path, actual_width_px, actual_height_px, fps, &ctx->encoder);
90   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
91     log_error("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
92     term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
93     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
94     return err;
95   }
96   log_debug("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder created successfully");
97 
98   // Initialize audio fields
99   ctx->audio_sample_rate = 48000;  // Audio pipeline is 48kHz
100   // Allocate large buffer for snapshot mode (5 seconds at 48kHz with ~14 FPS = 3,428 samples per frame)
101   // Use 8192 to comfortably handle snapshot reads without excessive overhead
102   ctx->audio_buf_size = 8192;      // Temporary buffer for reading audio (in floats)
103   ctx->audio_read_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->audio_buf_size * sizeof(float), float *);
104   ctx->audio_media_source = NULL;
105   ctx->audio_capture_rb = NULL;
106 
107   log_info("renderer: initialized encoder for %s", output_path);
108 
109   // Register render file context for debugging
110   NAMED_REGISTER_CONTEXT(ctx, "render_file_ctx", output_path, NULL);
111 
112   *out = ctx;
113   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
114 }

asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx)


Definition at line 310 of file renderer.c.


310                                                               {
311   if (!ctx)
312     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
313   asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
314   term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
315   SAFE_FREE(ctx->audio_read_buf);
316   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
317   return err;
318 }

asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx, const char * ansi_frame, uint64_t
  captured_ns)


Definition at line 123 of file renderer.c.


123                                                                                                                 {
124   // Write to debug file directly to bypass logging system
125   FILE *dbg = fopen("/tmp/render-debug.txt", "a");
126   if (dbg) {
127     fprintf(dbg, "[RENDER_WRITE_FRAME] Called with ctx=%p, frame_len=%zu, captured_ns=%llu\n", (void *)ctx,
128             ansi_frame ? strlen(ansi_frame) : 0, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
129     fclose(dbg);
130   }
131   log_info("render_file_write_frame: CALLED - ctx=%p, captured_ns=%llu", (void *)ctx, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
132 
133   if (!ctx) {
134     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ctx is NULL");
135     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
136   }
137 
138   if (!ansi_frame) {
139     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ansi_frame is NULL");
140     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
141   }
142 
143   size_t frame_len = strlen(ansi_frame);
144   log_info("render_file_write_frame: processing frame (len=%zu)", frame_len);
145   if (frame_len > 0) {
146     log_info("  first 100 chars: %.100s", ansi_frame);
147   }
148 
149   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_feed(ctx->renderer, ansi_frame, frame_len);
150   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
151     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: term_renderer_feed failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
152     return err;
153   }
154 
155   const uint8_t *pixels = term_renderer_pixels(ctx->renderer);
156   int pitch = term_renderer_pitch(ctx->renderer);
157   int width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
158   int height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
159 
160   log_info("render_file_write_frame: pixels=%p pitch=%d dims=%dx%d", (void *)pixels, pitch, width_px, height_px);
161 
162   // Check pixels in multiple locations to verify content
163   if (pixels) {
164     // Check pixel at (0,0) - top left corner
165     uint8_t sample_r = pixels[0], sample_g = pixels[1], sample_b = pixels[2];
166 
167     // Check pixel at (500, 100) where we injected a test red stripe
168     size_t test_offset = 100 * pitch + 500 * 3;
169     uint8_t test_r = pixels[test_offset], test_g = pixels[test_offset + 1], test_b = pixels[test_offset + 2];
170 
171     // Check pixel at middle of screen
172     uint8_t sample_r_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch], sample_g_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 1],
173             sample_b_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 2];
174 
175     log_info("  pixel[0,0]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), test_stripe[500,100]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), pixel[0,%d]: RGB(%u,%u,%u)",
176              sample_r, sample_g, sample_b, test_r, test_g, test_b, height_px/2, sample_r_mid, sample_g_mid, sample_b_mid);
177   }
178 
179   // CRITICAL: Copy pixel buffer before encoding
180   // term_renderer_pixels() returns a pointer to the renderer's internal buffer which gets
181   // overwritten on the next frame. We must copy the data before passing to the encoder.
182   uint8_t *pixels_copy = NULL;
183   if (pixels) {
184     size_t pixel_buffer_size = (size_t)pitch * (size_t)height_px;
185     pixels_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(pixel_buffer_size, uint8_t *);
186     memcpy(pixels_copy, pixels, pixel_buffer_size);
187     log_info("render_file_write_frame: copied %zu bytes of pixel data (was at %p, now at %p)",
188              pixel_buffer_size, (void *)pixels, (void *)pixels_copy);
189 
190     // DEBUG: Inject test red stripe to verify encoder is working
191     static int frame_num = 0;
192     if (frame_num++ < 1) {
193       log_info("DEBUG: Injecting test RED vertical stripe into pixel buffer for verification");
194       // Fill a 100-pixel-wide vertical stripe with bright red at x=500
195       for (int y = 0; y < height_px; y++) {
196         for (int x = 500; x < 600 && x < width_px; x++) {
197           uint8_t *pixel = pixels_copy + y * pitch + x * 3;
198           pixel[0] = 255;  // R
199           pixel[1] = 0;    // G
200           pixel[2] = 0;    // B
201         }
202       }
203     }
204   }
205 
206   err = ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(ctx->encoder, pixels_copy, pitch, captured_ns);
207   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
208     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
209   }
210 
211   // Free the pixel copy (ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame reads and converts it immediately)
212   SAFE_FREE(pixels_copy);
213 
214   // Write synchronized audio for this frame if available
215   // In snapshot mode, track actual FPS and calculate samples_per_frame dynamically
216   if (ctx && ctx->audio_read_buf && ctx->encoder) {
217     // Detect if we're in snapshot mode
218     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
219     double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
220 
221     int samples_per_frame;
222 
223     // Track frame timing for dynamic FPS calculation in snapshot mode
224     static uint64_t first_frame_ns = 0;
225     static int log_once = 0;
226 
227     if (first_frame_ns == 0) {
228       first_frame_ns = captured_ns;
229     }
230 
231     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
232       // Simple approach: distribute snapshot_delay * sample_rate evenly across expected ~70 frames
233       // This ensures total audio duration = snapshot_delay seconds
234       int total_target_samples = (int)(ctx->audio_sample_rate * snapshot_delay);
235       int expected_frame_count = (int)(13.6 * snapshot_delay);  // 13.6 FPS is typical capture rate
236       samples_per_frame = total_target_samples / expected_frame_count;
237 
238       // Ensure minimum to avoid rounding to 0
239       if (samples_per_frame < 100) {
240         samples_per_frame = 100;
241       }
242 
243     } else {
244       // Normal/live mode: use 60 FPS baseline
245       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_sample_rate / 60;
246       if (!log_once++) {
247         log_info("[AUDIO_CALC] Normal mode: samples_per_frame=%d", samples_per_frame);
248       }
249     }
250 
251     if (samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
252       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
253       if (!log_once++) {
254         log_warn("[AUDIO_CALC] samples_per_frame clamped to buffer size %d", ctx->audio_buf_size);
255       }
256     }
257     if (samples_per_frame <= 0) {
258       samples_per_frame = 800;  // Fallback minimum
259     }
260 
261     int samples_read = 0;
262     static int audio_eof_reached = 0;
263 
264     // Read from whichever audio source is available (prefer media source, fallback to capture)
265     if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_media_source) {
266       // Read from media source (file/URL audio) - try to get samples
267       samples_read = media_source_read_audio(ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
268 
269       if (samples_read <= 0) {
270         // EOF reached or error - no more audio samples available from source
271         audio_eof_reached = 1;
272         samples_read = 0;
273       }
274     } else if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_capture_rb) {
275       // Read from ring buffer (live mic capture)
276       samples_read =
277           audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->audio_capture_rb, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
278       if (samples_read < 0) {
279         samples_read = 0;  // On error, write silence
280         audio_eof_reached = 1;
281       } else if (samples_read == 0) {
282         audio_eof_reached = 1;
283       }
284       log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from capture_rb (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
285                 samples_per_frame, samples_read);
286     }
287 
288     // Write audio: either from source or silence to fill remaining time
289     if (samples_read > 0) {
290       // We got samples from the source - write them
291       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_read);
292     } else {
293       // No samples available (EOF reached or not available) - write silence to maintain sync
294       // This ensures audio track duration matches video track duration
295       memset(ctx->audio_read_buf, 0, samples_per_frame * sizeof(float));
296       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
297     }
298 
299   }
300 
301   return err;
302 }

    render_file_ctx_s::audio_buf_size,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_capture_rb,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_media_source,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_read_buf, audio_ring_buffer_read(),
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_sample_rate,
    render_file_ctx_s::encoder, ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(), GET_OPTION, log_debug,
    log_info, log_once, log_warn,
    media_source_read_audio(), render_file_ctx_s::renderer,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, term_renderer_feed(),
    term_renderer_height_px(), term_renderer_pitch(),
